pages.raw_text:
The resolutions committee rec.


ommend amendment of the home


stead law suspending the resi-.


dence requirement until the gov


eminent is able to furnish water


under irrigation projects; issu'


ance Of $4,000, 900 five year bonds


to augment the reclamation fund /;


modification of the lien foreclosure


process; unificatiOn of the differ.
ent federal agents under reclama.
ton measures into one agency; ex.
tension OF reclamation service to
Hawaii; regulation Of water re-


serve, timber cutting, timber taxa
tion; affirming peoples right to
water and denying rights to grant
franchises for water use in per
petuity; urge forest reserve meas


ures, naming the Appalachian prob
lem; giving credit for reclamation
accomplished under private pro-
jects, and urging census to be
taken of this work; commencing
the Audubon society's campaign In
behalf of birds, and thanking city
and state and their officers and
local officials Of the congress fol-
the splendid manner in which the
congress has been entertained In


this city.


The resolutions mentioned the
Chicago drainage canal as one OF
the projects to be fostered, and an
effort was made to also include the
Columbia, but the motion lost on
the ground that the congress was


already committed to this improve


ment along general lines.


A representative of the Farmers
union opposed the bond issue, but
failed in his efforts to amend.
